title: Single Soul, Two Bodies
quote: Love is composed of a single soul inhabiting two bodies.
line: Aristotle's geometry of devotion says twoness can house one spirit without losing edges.
alt: Two friends bundled up beneath an overpass flash peace signs during a cold night walk.

title: Twin Hearts
quote: Two souls dwell, alas, in my breast, and each one wants to separate from the other.
line: Goethe names the tug-of-war inside every body built for two impulses.
alt: Twin hearts carved into a weathered limestone block catch the afternoon light.

title: Infinite Seconds
quote: Every second is of infinite value.
line: Goethe upgrades each tick into a sunrise you can stand inside.
alt: Macro close-up of crimson numerals against a bright green enamel panel.

title: Timekeepers
quote: The two most powerful warriors are patience and time.
line: Tolstoy crowns the two forces that make every second chapter possible.
alt: A numbered paper deli ticket marked 222 rests in the palm of a hand.

title: Yielding Victories
quote: When two great forces oppose each other, the victory will go to the one that knows how to yield.
line: Taoist math: the softer two wins over the rigid two.
alt: Close-up of a bold white numeral painted across a red metal surface.

title: Forked Paths
quote: Two roads diverged in a wood, and I— I took the one less traveled by, and that has made all the difference.
line: If there are always two routes, Frost selects the rebel seam.
alt: Tilted view of a red and white speed limit sign framed by bare tree branches.

title: Candle & Mirror
quote: There are two ways to spread light: to be the candle or the mirror that reflects it.
line: Wharton hands us a binary for service: emit or reflect.
alt: Blue awning typography with the digits 125 cropped at an angle.

title: Starry Law
quote: Two things fill the mind with ever new and increasing admiration and awe, the starry heavens above me and the moral law within me.
line: Kant's paired infinities keep us looking up and inward simultaneously.
alt: Yellow license plate crop focusing on a glossy black number two.

title: Miracle Modes
quote: There are only two ways to live your life. One is as though nothing is a miracle. The other is as though everything is a miracle.
line: Einstein's slider for awe is binary: mute it or saturate it.
alt: Neon green window decal showing the number two inside a circle.

title: Mud or Stars
quote: Two men look out through the same bars: one sees the mud, and one the stars.
line: Langbridge reminds us that twoness also means perspective splits.
alt: Layered blue numerals reflected in a storefront pane.

title: Flint Sparks
quote: It takes two flints to make a fire.
line: Alcott's rule says progress needs the strike of two willing edges.
alt: Distressed black stenciled numerals on brushed metal.

title: Twin Solitudes
quote: Love consists of two solitudes that protect and border and greet each other.
line: Rilke keeps the twos close enough to bow, far enough to keep their outlines.
alt: Massive patterned letters and numerals installed in an airy lobby.

title: We Two, Multiplied
line: Ovid insists that even two can sound like a crowd when the harmony lands.
alt: Two friends at a cocktail table pose with peace signs under warm lights.

title: Two Abysses
quote: We are two abysses— a well staring at the sky.
line: Pessoa turns twoness into an echo chamber between earth and infinite blue.
alt: Collage of overlapping numerals and signage textures in blue and chartreuse.
